No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description or case list supports a connection to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course description or case list supports a connection to ending hunger or improving nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course includes cases like 'Sell v. United States' (forced medication) and 'BVerwG zu selbstbestimmtem Sterben' (self-determined dying), which directly relate to legal rights concerning mental health and well-being (Target 3.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course examines constitutional judgments on 'cruel and unusual punishment' and warrantless searches, which are fundamental to ensuring equal protection under the law and reducing inequalities (Target 10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course description or case list relates to urban development, housing, or public spaces.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No evidence in the course description or case list relates to sustainable consumption, waste, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No evidence in the course description or case list relates to climate change or environmental protection.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course description or case list relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course description or case list relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's core subject is 'Criminal Procedure Law' and constitutional review by supreme courts, directly supporting the rule of law, access to justice (Target 16.3), and effective, accountable institutions (Target 16.6).
